zh.Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England: 英國越可爾桑頓 在 克雷文
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Lister於1512年出生在英國越可爾桑頓時，他的父親Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope26歲，母親Lady Eleanor Clayton12歲。他於1534年與Anne Midhope在英國英格蘭越可爾吉斯本結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1582年10月4日在英國越可爾桑頓 在 克雷文享年70歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉斯本。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Lister于1512年出生在英国越可爾桑頓时，他的父亲Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope26岁，母亲Lady Eleanor Clayton12岁。他于1534年与Anne Midhope在英国英格兰越可爾吉斯本结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1582年10月4日在英国越可爾桑頓 在 克雷文享年70岁，埋葬在英国越可爾吉斯本。

nameMeanings.name: Lister
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a dyer of cloth, Middle English li(te)ster, a derivative of Middle English lit(t)en (Old Norse lita) ‘to dye’. This term was used principally in East Anglia and northern and eastern England (areas of Scandinavian settlement), and to this day the surname is found principally in these regions, especially in Yorkshire.

variants.name: Lester
meanings: English: habitational name from Leicester, which is recorded as Ligeraceastre in the 10th century. The placename derives from an Old English folk name Legore ‘the dwellers by the river Legor (a lost river name)’ + Old English ceaster ‘city, Roman fortification’.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lestre in Normandy.
English and Scottish: variant of   Lister  .

variants.name: Lyster
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Lister  .
Norwegian: habitational name from the farm name Ljøsta in Romsdal, probably derived from Old Norse ljóstr ‘fishing spear, leister’.
Danish: nickname originally from Bornholm, derived from a word for a fishing tackle for catching eel.

variants.name: Lidster
meanings: English: occupational name for a dyer, Middle Englishlitster (see Lister ).

variants.name: Litster
meanings: English: occupational name for a dyer, Middle Englishlitster (see Lister ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: When William Lister was born in 1512, in Thornton, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope, was 26 and his mother, Lady Eleanor Clayton, was 12. He married Anne Midhope in 1534, in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 4 October 1582, in Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Lister est né en 1512, à Thornton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lady Eleanor Clayton, avait 12 ans. Il a épousé Anne Midhope en 1534, à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 4 octobre 1582, à Thornton in Craven, Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England,: Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als William Lister 1512 in Thornton, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope 26 und seine Mutter Lady Eleanor Clayton 12. Er hat 1534 in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Anne Midhope geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 4. Oktober 1582 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Lister è nato il nel 1512 nel Thornton, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Lady Eleanor Clayton, aveva 12 anni. Lui sposato Anne Midhope nel 1534, nel Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 4 ottobre 1582, nel Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Gisburn, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Listerが1512年にイギリスヨークシュアイアソルントンで生まれた時，父Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhopeは26歳，母Lady Eleanor Claytonは12歳であった。1534年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンでAnne Midhopeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1582年10月4日にイギリスヨークシュアイアソルントン イン クラベンで70歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンにて埋葬された。

ko.Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England,: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 토른턴 인 크레이븐
ko.lifeSummary: William Lister(이)가 1512년 영국 요르크실르 토른턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Eleanor Clayton(은)는 12세였다. 그는 1534년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 지스번에서 Anne Midhope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1582년 10월 4일 영국 요르크실르 토른턴 인 크레이븐에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 지스번에 묻혔다.

pt.Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England,: Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Lister nasceu em 1512, em Thornton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lady Eleanor Clayton, tinha 12 anos. Ele casou-se com Anne Midhope em 1534, em Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 4 de outubro de 1582, em Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, England,: таурнтон ин кравен, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Lister родился в 1512 г. в местности таурнтон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Eleanor Clayton, 12. Он женился на женщине по имени Anne Midhope в 1534 г. в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 4 октября 1582 г. в местности таурнтон ин кравен, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Lister nació en 1512, en Thornton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Christopher Lister Esq of Medhope, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lady Eleanor Clayton, tenía 12 años. Se casó con Anne Midhope en 1534, en Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 4 de octubre de 1582, en Thornton in Craven, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
